message:
Add advanced filtering, bulk operations, and auto-refresh to viewer UI This commit transforms the viewer from a simple message browser into a feature-rich email client with Gmail-style advanced filtering, bulk message operations, auto-refresh capabilities, and enhanced visual feedback. These additions make the viewer significantly more powerful for managing large mailboxes and coordinating across multiple projects. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ File Changes Summary ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ **File**: src/mcp_agent_mail/viewer_assets/index.html - OLD: 468 lines (commit a45ed79) - NEW: 826 lines (this commit) - Added: 445 lines - Removed: 87 lines - Net change: +358 lines (+76% growth) This is the second major enhancement to the viewer UI, following: - Commit a45ed79: Initial transformation to Alpine.js + Tailwind - Commit a025b0c: Added Alpine.js controllers - This commit: Advanced features and interactions ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 1: Advanced Filtering System [lines 237-387] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Problem ------- Users with multi-project mailboxes containing hundreds or thousands of messages need ways to quickly filter down to relevant conversations. Basic search is helpful but insufficient for common workflows like: - "Show me all messages from BlueMountain about project-alpha" - "Show me all urgent messages" - "Show me all messages I sent to GreenCastle" - "Show me only threaded discussions" Solution: Gmail-Style Advanced Filters --------------------------------------- **Filters Toggle Button** [lines 237-245]: ```html ``` Features: - Toggles collapsible filters panel - Visual indicator (pulsing dot) when filters are active - Sticky positioning so always accessible when scrolling **Collapsible Filters Panel** [lines 307-387]: ```html
``` Smooth animations: - Slides down from top with fade-in - Slides up and fades out when closed - 200ms entrance, 150ms exit (feels snappy) **Filter Options**: 1. **Project Filter** [lines 318-328]: ```html ``` - Dynamically populated from messages - Example: project-alpha, backend-service, frontend-app - Filters to messages in selected project only 2. **Sender Filter** [lines 330-339]: ```html ``` - Dynamically populated from agents - Example: BlueMountain, GreenCastle, RedFox - Filters to messages sent by selected agent 3. **Recipient Filter** [lines 341-350]: ```html ``` - Dynamically populated from recipients - Filters to messages addressed to selected agent - Useful for finding "messages I received from X" 4. **Importance Filter** [lines 352-361]: ```html ``` - Standard importance levels - Quickly find critical messages - "urgent" + "high" are highlighted in UI 5. **Thread Filter** [lines 363-376]: ```html ``` - Separate threaded conversations from standalone messages - "Threaded Only": Messages with explicit thread_id - "Non-threaded": Standalone messages **Clear Filters Button** [lines 378-386]: ```html ``` - Resets all filters to default ("All ...") - Restores full message list - One-click reset for quick exploration Filter Combinations ------------------- Filters work together with AND logic: Example workflow: ``` 1. User selects filters: - Project: "backend-service" - Sender: "BlueMountain" - Importance: "urgent" 2. filterMessages() runs with all constraints: filteredMessages = allMessages.filter(msg => msg.project === "backend-service" && msg.sender === "BlueMountain" && msg.importance === "urgent" ) 3. Message list shows only matches (e.g., 3 messages) 4. Message count updates: "3 messages" ``` This enables powerful queries without complex search syntax. Grid Layout Responsive Design ------------------------------ ```html
``` Breakpoints: - Mobile (< 768px): 2 columns (Project, Sender stacked) - Tablet (768px - 1024px): 4 columns (2 rows of filters) - Desktop (> 1024px): 6 columns (single row + clear button) All filters always visible, just reflow for screen size. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 2: Bulk Message Operations [lines 407-437, 502-510] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Problem ------- Users managing hundreds of messages need bulk actions: - Mark 50 unread messages as read - Select all messages from a project for analysis - Quickly clear notification backlog Selecting messages one by one is tedious and error-prone. Solution: Gmail-Style Bulk Selection ------------------------------------- **Select All Checkbox** [lines 410-422]: ```html ``` Three visual states: 1. **Unchecked** (□): - No messages selected - Click: Select all visible messages 2. **Indeterminate** (⊟): - Some messages selected, but not all - Visual feedback that partial selection exists - Click: Select all remaining messages 3. **Checked** (☑): - All visible messages selected - Click: Deselect all messages Indeterminate State Logic: ```javascript $el.indeterminate = selectedMessages.length > 0 && selectedMessages.length < filteredMessages.length; ``` Examples: - 0 selected, 50 visible: □ (unchecked) - 10 selected, 50 visible: ⊟ (indeterminate) - 50 selected, 50 visible: ☑ (checked) Reactive Watchers: ```javascript $watch('selectedMessages', updateIndeterminate); $watch('filteredMessages', updateIndeterminate); ``` Automatically updates checkbox state when: - User selects/deselects individual messages - User changes filters (filteredMessages changes) - User clears selection **Selection Counter** [lines 423-436]: ```html ``` Appears only when messages are selected: - Shows count: "3 selected", "15 selected" - Animated entrance (slide-in) - Provides action buttons **Mark Read Button**: - Green success color - Icon: check mark - Action: Marks all selected messages as read in database - Use case: Bulk acknowledge notification backlog **Clear Button**: - Gray neutral color - Action: Clears selection (selectedMessages = []) - Quick escape from selection mode **Individual Message Checkboxes** [lines 504-509]: ```html ``` Smart visibility: - Default: Hidden (opacity-0) - On hover: Visible (group-hover:opacity-100) - When selected: Always visible (opacity-100) This keeps UI clean while making selection discoverable. Click handling: ```html @click.stop ``` - Prevents click from bubbling to message row - Allows checkbox toggle without opening message - User can check box OR click row to open Selection Logic --------------- **Toggle Single Message**: ```javascript toggleMessageSelection(msgId) { if (selectedMessages.includes(msgId)) { selectedMessages = selectedMessages.filter(id => id !== msgId); } else { selectedMessages = [...selectedMessages, msgId]; } } ``` **Toggle All Messages**: ```javascript toggleSelectAll() { if (selectedMessages.length === filteredMessages.length) { selectedMessages = []; // All selected: deselect all } else { selectedMessages = filteredMessages.map(msg => msg.id); // Select all visible } } ``` **Mark Selected as Read**: ```javascript markSelectedAsRead() { for (const msgId of selectedMessages) { // Update database: SET read = true WHERE id = msgId // Update UI: Remove unread styling } selectedMessages = []; // Clear selection after action } ``` Workflow Example ---------------- 1. User applies filters: Project = "backend", Importance = "urgent" 2. 25 messages match 3. User clicks select-all checkbox 4. All 25 messages get checkmarks 5. Counter shows "25 selected" 6. User clicks "Mark Read" 7. All 25 messages marked as read in database 8. Selection cleared automatically 9. Unread badges removed from list items This workflow takes ~5 seconds instead of 25 individual clicks. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 3: Auto-Refresh System [lines 273-298] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Problem ------- Static exports become stale as agents continue working. Users need fresh data to see latest messages, but don't want to manually reload the page constantly. Solution: Periodic Auto-Refresh -------------------------------- **Refresh Button** [lines 275-283]: ```html ``` Features: - Manual refresh on demand - Animated spinner during fetch - Color changes: gray → blue when refreshing - Accessible: aria-busy attribute for screen readers Spinner animation: ```css .animate-spin { animation: spin 1s linear infinite; } @keyframes spin { from { transform: rotate(0deg); } to { transform: rotate(360deg); } } ``` **Last Refresh Timestamp** [lines 284-289]: ```html
``` Displays: - Success: "Last updated: 2 minutes ago" - Error: "Failed to fetch: Network error" Examples: - "Just now" - "30 seconds ago" - "2 minutes ago" - "Failed to fetch: 404" **Auto-Refresh Toggle** [lines 290-297]: ```html ``` When enabled: - Automatically fetches latest messages every 30 seconds - Updates lastRefreshLabel - Shows spinner briefly during each fetch - User can continue browsing (non-blocking) When disabled: - Stops automatic fetching - User can still manually refresh - Preference saved in localStorage Auto-Refresh Implementation ---------------------------- **State Management**: ```javascript data() { return { autoRefreshEnabled: false, // Checkbox state isRefreshing: false, // Currently fetching refreshInterval: null, // setInterval handle lastRefresh: null, // Timestamp of last successful refresh refreshError: null, // Error message if failed }; } ``` **Toggle Handler**: ```javascript handleAutoRefreshToggle() { if (this.autoRefreshEnabled) { // Start auto-refresh this.refreshInterval = setInterval(() => { this.fetchLatestMessages(); }, 30000); // 30 seconds localStorage.setItem('autoRefreshEnabled', 'true'); } else { // Stop auto-refresh clearInterval(this.refreshInterval); this.refreshInterval = null; localStorage.setItem('autoRefreshEnabled', 'false'); } } ``` **Fetch Logic**: ```javascript async fetchLatestMessages() { this.isRefreshing = true; this.refreshError = null; try { // Fetch manifest to check if database changed const manifest = await loadJSON("../manifest.json"); // Compare manifest.database.sha256 with cached hash if (manifest.database.sha256 !== this.cachedDatabaseHash) { // Database changed: reload it const { bytes, source } = await loadDatabaseBytes(manifest); state.db = new state.SQL.Database(bytes); // Rebuild message list this.allMessages = this.getAllMessages(); this.filterMessages(); this.cachedDatabaseHash = manifest.database.sha256; } this.lastRefresh = new Date(); this.lastRefreshLabel = "Just now"; } catch (error) { console.error('[Refresh] Failed', error); this.refreshError = `Failed to fetch: ${error.message}`; } finally { this.isRefreshing = false; } } ``` Efficiency: - Only reloads database if sha256 hash changed - Avoids unnecessary processing if no new messages - Small manifest fetch (~1KB) checks for updates **Timestamp Formatting**: ```javascript // Update every 5 seconds setInterval(() => { if (this.lastRefresh) { const diffSeconds = Math.floor((new Date() - this.lastRefresh) / 1000); if (diffSeconds < 60) { this.lastRefreshLabel = "Just now"; } else if (diffSeconds < 3600) { const minutes = Math.floor(diffSeconds / 60); this.lastRefreshLabel = `${minutes} minute${minutes > 1 ? 's' : ''} ago`; } else { const hours = Math.floor(diffSeconds / 3600); this.lastRefreshLabel = `${hours} hour${hours > 1 ? 's' : ''} ago`; } } }, 5000); ``` Live updates without re-fetching. Use Cases --------- **Scenario 1**: Monitoring Active Collaboration - Multiple agents working on a project - User wants to see messages as they arrive - Enables auto-refresh - Viewer updates every 30s with new messages - User stays informed without manual refreshing **Scenario 2**: Checking for Responses - User sent an urgent message - Waiting for reply - Clicks manual refresh every few minutes - "Last updated: 1 minute ago" confirms freshness **Scenario 3**: Offline/Cached Viewing - User browsing old mailbox export - No new messages expected - Auto-refresh disabled (default) - Avoids unnecessary network requests ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 4: Enhanced Message List UI [lines 486-569] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ The message list rendering received major visual and functional enhancements. **Enhanced Message Item** [lines 488-569]: Old structure (commit a45ed79): ```html
A
Sender Time

Subject

``` New structure (this commit): ```html
BlueMountain → GreenCastle, RedFox
Project: backend-service Urgent Thread
Planning next sprint

Let's review the roadmap...

2 hours ago Unread 3 attachments
``` **New: Sender → Recipients Display** [lines 513-518]: ```html
``` Shows message flow: - "BlueMountain → GreenCastle" - "SystemBot → All Agents" - "You → BlueMountain, RedFox" Helps quickly identify message context. **New: Badges Row** [lines 520-551]: 1. **Project Badge** [lines 522-528]: ```html ``` Color-coded by project: - backend-service: Blue badge - frontend-app: Green badge - infrastructure: Purple badge - Each project gets consistent color (hash-based) 2. **Importance Badges** [lines 530-542]: ```html ``` Visual hierarchy: - Urgent: Red badge with alert-circle icon - High: Orange badge with alert-triangle icon - Normal: No badge (default) - Low: No badge (default) Only shows badges for elevated importance, keeps UI clean. 3. **Thread Indicator** [lines 544-550]: ```html ``` Shows when message is part of thread conversation. Helps distinguish standalone messages from discussions. **New: Preview Snippet** [line 556]: ```html

``` First 280 characters of message body: - "Let's review the roadmap and prioritize the next sprint..." - Helps user decide if message is relevant - Truncated with ellipsis if longer **New: Metadata Row** [lines 558-569]: ```html
``` Displays: - **Timestamp**: "2 hours ago", "Yesterday", "Oct 28" - **Unread indicator**: Blue "Unread" label if not read - **Attachments**: Paperclip icon + count if present **Staggered Animation** [line 497]: ```html :style="`animation-delay: ${Math.min(index * 0.02, 0.5)}s;`" ``` Messages animate in sequentially: - Message 1: 0ms delay - Message 2: 20ms delay - Message 3: 40ms delay - ... - Message 25+: 500ms delay (capped) Creates elegant cascading entrance effect. **Accessibility Improvements**: ```html tabindex="0" role="button" :aria-label="'Message from ' + msg.sender + ': ' + msg.subject" @keydown.enter="handleMessageClick(msg)" ``` - Keyboard navigable (Tab to focus, Enter to open) - Screen reader announces: "Message from BlueMountain: Planning next sprint" - ARIA role indicates interactive element **Visual States**: ```html :class="{ 'bg-primary-50 border-l-4 border-l-primary-500': selectedMessage && selectedMessage.id === msg.id, 'border-l-4 border-l-transparent hover:bg-slate-50': !selectedMessage || selectedMessage.id !== msg.id, 'font-semibold': !msg.read }" ``` States: - **Selected**: Blue background + blue left border - **Hover**: Light gray background - **Unread**: Bold font weight - **Default**: White background, transparent border ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 5: Improved Search UX [lines 207-232] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Search enhancements for better discoverability and usability. **Enhanced Search Input** [lines 214-221]: Old placeholder: ```html placeholder="Search all messages..." ``` New placeholder: ```html placeholder="Search all messages across all projects and agents..." ``` More descriptive: - Clarifies search scope (all projects, all agents) - Helps users understand search power - Encourages broad exploration **Keyboard Shortcut Hint** [lines 222-225]: ```html ⌘K ``` Displays: - Desktop: "⌘K" badge in search box - Mobile: Hidden (too small) Benefits: - Teaches users keyboard shortcut - Mimics Gmail, Slack, VS Code patterns - Professional appearance Future implementation: ```javascript // In JavaScript controller document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'k') { e.preventDefault(); document.getElementById('unified-search').focus(); } }); ``` **Clear Search Button Enhancement** [lines 211-220]: Old: ```html ``` New: ```html ``` Changes: - x-show → x-if: Removes from DOM when hidden (cleaner) - aria-label: Screen reader accessibility - Better spacing with absolute positioning **Search Box ID**: ```html id="unified-search" ``` Enables: - Keyboard shortcut focus targeting - URL fragment linking (#unified-search) - Test automation targeting - Label association ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 6: Enhanced Sort UI [lines 439-472] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Sorting redesigned as dropdown menu instead of inline buttons. **Sort Dropdown Button** [lines 441-445]: ```html ``` Shows current sort: - "Newest" (default) - "Oldest" - "Sender" Icon: arrows-down-up (universal sort symbol) **Dropdown Menu** [lines 447-472]: ```html
``` Features: - Smooth open/close animation (100ms) - Click outside to close (@click.outside) - Active item highlighted (blue background) - Icons for each sort option - Executes filterMessages() + closes menu on select Space savings: - Old: 3 buttons × 100px = 300px horizontal space - New: 1 button = 100px horizontal space - Saves: 200px for other controls ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 7: View Mode Refinements [lines 247-261] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ View mode toggle redesigned for clarity. Old View Modes (commit a45ed79): ```html ``` Confusing labels: - "inbox" could mean unread messages - "threads" ambiguous (threaded view? thread list?) New View Modes (this commit): ```html ``` Clear semantics: - **Split view**: List + detail pane (Gmail style) - **List view**: List only (hide detail) Icons: - columns-2: Two columns (split pane visualization) - list: Bullet list (list-only visualization) ARIA labels explain purpose for screen readers. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 8: Message Count Display [lines 300-304] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Shows filtered message count for context. ```html
``` Displays: - "1 message" - "25 messages" - "0 messages" Updates reactively: - User searches: "125 messages" → "3 messages" - User filters: "125 messages" → "8 messages" - User clears: "8 messages" → "125 messages" Provides immediate feedback on filter effectiveness. Hidden on mobile (md:flex): - Too small for additional info - Space needed for controls ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 9: Empty State Improvements [lines 478-483] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Better empty state when no messages match filters. ```html ``` Old: Blank space when no results New: - Large inbox icon (16×16, 64px actual) - Centered text: "No messages found" - Gray colors (not alarming) Helps user understand: - Filters are working (not broken UI) - No matches for current criteria - Prompt to adjust filters ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 10: Sticky Positioning Enhancements [lines 204, 315] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Search and filters stay accessible while scrolling. **Search Bar** [line 204]: ```html
``` - Sticks 64px from top (below navigation) - Z-index 20 (above content, below modals) - Shadow appears when stuck (visual separation) **Filters Panel** [line 315]: ```html
``` - Sticks 160px from top (below search bar) - Z-index 10 (below search, above content) - Only sticky when visible Combined effect: ``` [Navigation] ← Always fixed top [Search Bar] ← Sticky at top-16 [Filters Panel] ← Sticky at top-40 (when open) [Message List] ← Scrollable content ``` User can: - Scroll through hundreds of messages - Always access search - Always access filters (when open) - Never lose navigation ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Feature 11: Fullscreen Z-Index Management [lines 205, 316, 400] ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Proper z-index stacking in fullscreen mode. ```html :class="isFullscreen ? 'z-50' : 'z-20'" ``` Normal mode z-index stack: ``` z-50: Modals, notifications z-30: Navigation z-20: Search bar z-10: Filters panel z-0: Content ``` Fullscreen mode z-index stack: ``` z-50: Search bar, filters, content (all elevated) z-30: Navigation (unchanged) z-0: Background (dimmed) ``` When fullscreen activated: 1. Backdrop overlay appears (z-40) 2. Search, filters, content elevated to z-50 3. All interactive elements remain accessible 4. Navigation stays visible (optional: could hide) Prevents: - Search bar hidden behind fullscreen content - Filters inaccessible in fullscreen - Z-index fighting between layers ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Technical Improvements ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ **Alpine.js Patterns**: 1. **x-if vs x-show**: ```html ``` Benefits: - Smaller DOM (better performance) - Cleaner inspector view - Forces full re-render on toggle (good for complex widgets) 2. **Click Outside Directive**: ```html
``` Built-in Alpine magic: - Detects clicks outside element - Closes dropdown/modal automatically - No manual event listener management 3. **Reactive Classes**: ```html :class="{ 'bg-primary-50': isSelected, 'hover:bg-slate-50': !isSelected, 'font-semibold': !msg.read }" ``` Multiple conditional classes in one binding. **Performance Optimizations**: 1. **Filter Limits**: ```html
